title: Marinated Pungent Argentinian Tofu Empanadas
description: A flavorful twist on traditional empanadas, featuring marinated tofu, infused oil, and a shojin-friendly twist.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: pressured for 30 minutes to remove excess moisture

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: minced and mixed with olive oil

ingredients.name: oregano
ingredients.notes: dried and mixed with olive oil

ingredients.name: cumin
ingredients.notes: ground and mixed with olive oil

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the minced garlic, dried oregano, ground cumin, and sugar.
Add the apple cider vinegar, salt, and 3 tablespoons of extra-virgin olive oil to the bowl and whisk until combined.
Cut the firm tofu into small cubes and add to the marinade, mixing until the tofu is well coated.
In a separate bowl, combine the all-purpose flour and 1/2 cup of water, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a few cubes of marinated tofu in the center of each dough circle, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges of the dough with the remaining olive oil and fold the dough over the tofu, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with the remaining olive oil.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
